qas.q:
Which of the following answers refer to common battery types that can be found in modern portable devices?

Nickel-Iron (Ni-Fe)
Lithium-ion (Li-ion)
Nickel-Metal Hydride (Ni-MH)
Lithium-ion Polymer (LiPo)
Nickel-Cadmium (Ni-Cd)

Which laptop features allows to overcome standard keyboard size restrictions?

Touchpad
Numeric keypad
Fn key
Digitizer

What is the most common memory module form factor type used in laptops?

ECC RAM
DIMM
SODIMM
CRIMM

Which of the answers listed below refer(s) to internal data storage device type(s) used in laptop computers?

Flash drives
USB drives
Magnetic disks
SSDs

Which of the following statements refer to the characteristic features of magnetic drives?

High capacity
Low device cost
High performance
Low capacity
High device cost
Low performance
qas.a:
High capacity
Low device cost
Low performance

qas.q:
Which of the answers listed below describe the features of Solid-State Drives (SSDs)? (Select 3 answers)

Low performance
Relatively high device cost
Lower capacity in comparison to magnetic drives
High performance
Relatively low device cost
Higher capacity in comparison to magnetic drives
qas.a:
Relatively high device cost

Lower capacity in comparison to magnetic drives

High performance

qas.q:
Which of the following hardware components would be the most difficult to replace on a laptop computer?

Optical drive
Primary storage
System board
Integrated GPU
Secondary storage

Which type of expansion card enables communication in 802.11 networks?

WLAN
Riser card
WPAN
WWAN

Which of the following answers refers to a laptop's Bluetooth module?

WWAN
WLAN
Riser card
WPAN

A type of expansion card that enables communication over cellular network is referred to as:

WPAN card
WAN card
WWAN card
WLAN card

In computer security, the term "Biometrics" refers to physical characteristics of the human body that can be used for identification and access control purposes.

True
False

Which of the following answers refers to the biometric authentication method implemented in modern laptops?

Fingerprint scan
Near-field scanner features
GPS reading
Face recognition
Authenticator app

A very short-range communication method where a wireless signal is sent between two devices that are touching or nearly touching each other is a characteristic feature of:

RFID
NFC
Infrared(IR)
Bluetooth

A technology implemented in modern laptops that enables data transfer and authentication features between the laptop and another portable device at a very close range is known as:

NFC
Wi-Fi
IoT
WiMAX

Which of the following answers refers to a display technology most commonly used in modern laptops?

LCD
OLED
Plasma
CRT

What are the characteristic features of In-Plane Switching (IPS)? (Select 3 answers)

High color quality
Fast response times
Wide viewing angles
Low color quality
Slow response times
Low viewing angles
qas.a:
High color quality

Wide viewing angles

Slow response times

qas.q:
Which of the following answers describe the Twisted Nematic (TN) technology? (Select 3 answers)

Low viewing angles
Fast response times
Low color quality
High color quality
Slow response times
Wide viewing angles
qas.a:
Low viewing angles

Fast response times

Low color quality

qas.q:
Vertical Alignment (VA) display technology can be characterized by: (Select 3 answers)

High contrast ratios
Low color quality
Good viewing angles
Low contrast ratios
Good color quality
Low viewing angles
qas.a:
High contrast ratios

Good viewing angles

Good color quality

qas.q:
What are the characteristic features of an OLED display? (Select 3 answers)

- Works without backlight
- Inferior contrast ratio and color representation in comparison to LCDs
- Lower light output (brightness) in comparison to LCDs
- Display type commonly used in modern laptop devices
- Better contrast ratio and color representation in comparison to LCDs
- Requires backlight to illuminate the screen
- Lower cost in comparison to LCDs
- Higher light output (brightness) in comparison to LCDs
qas.a:
Works without backlight

Lower light output (brightness) in comparison to LCDs

Better contrast ratio and color representation in comparison to LCDs

qas.q:
IPS, TN, VA, and OLED are all implementations of the LCD display technology.

True
False

A type of backlight technology most commonly used in modern laptop devices is known as:

CCFL
CRT
OLED
LED

Which of the answers listed below refers to the location of a laptop's Wi-Fi antenna?

On the WLAN module
Near the top, inside display case
Inside laptop's case
Typically attached via external expansion port
qas.a: Near the top, inside display case

qas.q:
Which of the following answers refers to a device capable of providing both input and output functions?

Microphone
Touchpad
Speaker
Touch screen
Monitor

Which of the following answers refer to the function of digitizer? (Select 3 answers)

- Translates analog data into a format suitable for computer processing
- A type of output device
- Component of a mobile device's screen that allows to control the device with a stylus or fingertip
- An example of media converter
- A type of input device
qas.a: Translates analog data into a format suitable for computer processing

qas.q:
Which of the answers listed below describe the function of a laptop's inverter? (Select 2 answers)

Converts AC power into DC power
Used for supplying voltage to backlights in older types of LCD panels
Converts DC power into AC power
Used for supplying voltage to backlights in OLED displays
qas.a:
Used for supplying voltage to backlights in older types of LCD panels

Converts DC power into AC power
